hypothesis: If language models initially SFT on BCC/B2 alloy triples are further tuned with DPO using Thermo-Calc–derived preference feedback under a hierarchical reward, then they will generate a higher fraction of valid, novel BCC/B2 compositions that meet coexistence, BCC-first solidification, room-temperature B2, and <10% impurity criteria with reduced lattice mismatch than SFT-only and prompt-based baselines because physics-based feedback aligns the model’s distribution with thermodynamically feasible designs.
